title: Performance Contention
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Performance Contention Concurrent processes might simultaneously allocate the same resources, such as certain parts of internal memory or specific rows of a table. Intensive use of the same resources by various processes cause contention and can decrease performance. In such situations, it is worthwhile to rearrange the application architecture so the required resources are allocated more selectively. An obvious way to do this is to reconsider the transaction definitions by minimizing the locking periods, the amount of locked data, or both. The following suggestions can also help reduce process contention: Attach components—use the /attach switch when you create instances of Uniface forms, services, and reports. This ensures that all the attached child instances of the component are removed before the current instance exits, thereby freeing system resources. Avoid using the read/lock ProcScript instruction—the read/lock instruction results in paranoid locking. It is recommended that you avoid using this instruction for online transactions, because so many locks are required. Minimize the size of transactions. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
